summary: LINQ operators for IAsyncEnumerable<T> when asynchronous pull-based streaming is the right abstraction.
recommendedPatterns: Use IAsyncEnumerable<T> when consumers should pull async results incrementally.
Apply LINQ operators from System.Linq.Async instead of manual async iterator loops when clarity improves.
Keep push-vs-pull decisions explicit at public API boundaries.
avoidPatterns: Do not replace broadcast live streams with IAsyncEnumerable<T> unless pull semantics are acceptable.
Do not bury cancellation and enumeration ownership.
setupSteps: Model backpressure and cancellation expectations explicitly.
Test enumeration order and cancellation behavior.
commonPitfalls: Using IAsyncEnumerable<T> for event fan-out scenarios that need multiple live observers.
Mixing pull and push pipelines without documenting the transition.
